panel_shape.positive_treatment_timing: must equal the last observed time period

supported_cck_backend_scope.sieve_selection: not implemented: no data-driven K-hat, Lepski, or npiv sieve selection is exposed by the runtime backend
supported_cck_backend_scope.adaptive_cck_status: unsupported boundary until a paper/reference-supported adaptive CCK backend contract lands

unsupported_cases.predicate: dose_est_method == 'cck' and panel has no positive treatment timing cohort
unsupported_cases.error: cck estimator requires exactly one positive treatment timing cohort

unsupported_cases.predicate: dose_est_method == 'cck' and panel has more than one positive treatment timing cohort
unsupported_cases.error: cck estimator not supported with staggered adoption yet

unsupported_cases.predicate: dose_est_method == 'cck' and panel has one treated cohort but more than two observed time periods
unsupported_cases.error: cck estimator not supported with more than two time periods. consider averaging across pre and post treatment periods

unsupported_cases.predicate: dose_est_method == 'cck' and panel has two observed periods with one positive treatment timing cohort that does not equal the last observed period
unsupported_cases.error: cck estimator requires positive treatment timing to start in the post period
supported (fixed-dimension CCK); adaptive Lepski NOT supported in event-study aggregation

unsupported_cases.predicate: dose_est_method == 'cck' and aggregation == 'eventstudy' and control_group is outside the checked public event-study set
unsupported_cases.error: must be one of the CCK panel-shape/event-study errors above, never a relaxed event-study control-group fit

non_goals: Do not implement CCK event-study estimation without a new paper/reference-supported estimator contract.
Do not infer staggered-adoption CCK support from the multi-period identification discussion alone; the paper does not provide formal multi-period CCK estimation results.

Do not claim full data-driven Lepski/npiv adaptive CCK parity on the current cck_polynomial_backend; it is a fixed quadratic polynomial scaffold until a paper/reference-supported adaptive backend lands.
Do not let base_period or control_group options relax the unsupported CCK boundary.
